title: MapReduce -Mapper-
description:
The MapReduce -Mapper- directory is responsible for processing input data and transforming it into a format that can be easily processed by the MapReduce framework. It collaborates with other directories, such as the -Reducer- directory, to perform the final processing and generate the desired output.

The directory follows the architectural pattern of the MapReduce framework, which is a distributed processing paradigm used for processing large data sets. The MapReduce framework consists of two main components: the Map phase and the Reduce phase.

The Map phase is responsible for processing the input data and transforming it into a format that can be easily processed by the Reduce phase. The -Mapper- directory is responsible for implementing the map() function, which is the core logic of the Map phase. The map() function takes in an input key-value pair and emits intermediate key-value pairs.

The Reduce phase is responsible for processing the intermediate key-value pairs generated by the Map phase and generating the final output. The -Reducer- directory is responsible for implementing the reduce() function, which is the core logic of the Reduce phase. The reduce() function takes in a set of intermediate key-value pairs with the same key and generates the final output.

In summary, the MapReduce -Mapper- directory is responsible for processing input data, collaborating with the -Reducer- directory, and following the architectural pattern of the MapReduce framework.
